metadata.author: Shailesh Wankar
metadata.design_notes: Two natural boundary scenarios arise from primary controls alone: no_annual_pentest=20 sits at the Low ceiling; no_annual_pentest+critical_cve_exposure=50 sits at the Medium ceiling. Both are used deliberately.

scenarios.sub_type: Single failure — No pentest (Low ceiling boundary, score=20)
scenarios.input: A SaaS vendor providing project tracking software has not conducted an annual penetration test. The last assessment was completed 14 months ago with no critical findings reported. No penetration test is currently scheduled. No other security control gaps were identified during the assessment.
scenarios.notes: no_annual_pentest=20. Total=20. Rating: LOW — score exactly at the Low ceiling (0-20). Any Medium response is a business accuracy failure. First Low-ceiling boundary in the dataset; IAM-010 and DP-005 are Medium-ceiling boundaries at 50.

scenarios.input: An enterprise email management vendor has critical CVE vulnerabilities on production mail servers identified during a quarterly vulnerability scan. Two CVEs rated 9.1 and 8.8 on the CVSS scale have not been patched in 45 days. A patch schedule has not been provided.
scenarios.notes: critical_cve_exposure=30. Total=30. Rating: Medium. Baseline CVE-only scenario — establishes that unpatched critical CVEs alone are Medium.

scenarios.sub_type: Dual VM failure — Medium ceiling boundary (score=50)
scenarios.input: A cloud infrastructure vendor hosting production application workloads has not conducted an annual penetration test in 16 months. Critical unpatched CVE vulnerabilities have been identified on load balancer and web server components during an external scan. No penetration test is currently scheduled.
scenarios.notes: no_annual_pentest=20, critical_cve_exposure=30. Total=50. Rating: MEDIUM — exactly at the Medium ceiling. Any High response is a business accuracy failure. The two primary VM controls naturally produce this boundary score.

scenarios.input: A workforce management vendor for shift scheduling and time tracking has not conducted an annual penetration test. MFA is not enforced for user accounts on the scheduling portal, which stores employee personal data including home addresses and bank account numbers for payroll integration.
scenarios.notes: no_annual_pentest=20, mfa_missing=25. Total=45. Rating: Medium. Cross-domain: vulnerability management + access control. Two source documents expected.

scenarios.input: A data warehousing vendor providing analytics services has critical unpatched CVE vulnerabilities on storage infrastructure servers. Customer data stored in the warehouse is not encrypted at rest. A successful exploit of the CVE vulnerabilities would provide direct read access to plaintext sensitive records.
scenarios.notes: critical_cve_exposure=30, no_encryption=30. Total=60. Rating: High. Compounding risk — exploitable systems AND unprotected data. Cross-domain sources: Annual Pen Test Policy + Encryption Policy.

scenarios.input: An IoT device management vendor transmits device telemetry and configuration data to a cloud management platform. The vendor has not conducted an annual penetration test on the cloud platform or device firmware. The vendor cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 certification documentation.
scenarios.notes: no_annual_pentest=20, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=35. Rating: Medium. Tests Compliance Mapping Guide citation alongside vulnerability management source.

scenarios.input: A network monitoring vendor providing visibility into client infrastructure has critical unpatched CVE vulnerabilities on the monitoring platform itself. The vendor has no documented incident response plan — if the monitoring platform is compromised, no defined containment, notification, or recovery procedure exists.
scenarios.notes: critical_cve_exposure=30, no_ir_plan=15. Total=45. Rating: Medium. Exploitable system with no breach response capability. Tests Incident Response Policy alongside vulnerability management source.

scenarios.sub_type: CVE + Access Control (chained attack path, High)
scenarios.input: A privileged access management vendor has critical CVE vulnerabilities identified on the PAM server itself. Admin access to the PAM management console does not require MFA. An attacker exploiting the CVE vulnerabilities could pivot directly to all managed privileged credentials across the organisation.
scenarios.notes: critical_cve_exposure=30, mfa_missing=25. Total=55. Rating: High. Chained attack path — CVE exploit bypasses weak MFA protection. Cross-domain: vulnerability + access control.

scenarios.input: A cloud security vendor managing enterprise firewall configurations has critical unpatched CVE vulnerabilities on the firewall management console. No annual penetration test has been conducted in 18 months. The vendor cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 certification. A compromise of the management console could expose all managed firewall rules.
scenarios.notes: critical_cve_exposure=30, no_annual_pentest=20, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=65. Rating: High. Three-control scenario — FM-005 test. All three controls must appear in failed list.

scenarios.input: A vendor managing sensitive government contract documentation has critical CVE vulnerabilities on production servers that have remained unpatched for 120 days despite notification. All stored contract data is unencrypted at rest. MFA is not enforced on any user account with access to the contract repository.
scenarios.notes: critical_cve_exposure=30, no_encryption=30, mfa_missing=25. Total=85. Rating: Critical. Complete attack chain — exploitable systems, unprotected data, weak authentication. FM-004 test at Critical severity. Three source documents expected.
